title: Walking with Jesus: The Gospels, On Repeat, To Be Shaped Like Christ
author: Walking with Jesus Ministries
description: Jesus told of a man who swept his house clean, only for seven worse spirits to move in — an empty house isn't enough; it needs to be filled. This podcast is part of that filling: fifteen minutes a day with the actual words of Christ, straight from the King James Version. Some days a single Gospel front to back, some days all four woven into one story. No commentary — just Jesus, the center of scripture, still knocking to enter daily life, not just Sundays. Move through the Gospels several times each year. Fifteen minutes. Five days a week. On repeat. Let Him in to fill your house.

title: Emily, lately
author: Emily
description: Every episode is an honest conversation about real life, personal growth, relationships, work, books, mental clutter, and whatever else has been on my mind lately. Some weeks we’ll laugh, some weeks we’ll get a little vulnerable, but every week you’ll know you’re not alone.

title: Smile Pulse
author: Ash Toub
description: The honest playbook for running a dental practice. Ash Toub has owned and operated hundreds of dental practices, overseen hundreds of millions in production, and worked with 500+ providers. Each week he breaks down one pillar of building, running, or scaling a practice — revenue cycle, front office, clinical ops, marketing, facilities, leadership, and everything in between. No fluff. Real numbers, real lessons, real fights. If you own a practice, want to, or work inside one, this is the show that tells you what they never taught you in dental school.

title: GLIMMERS of Another Way of Being
author: Stephanie MacKinnon
description:
The world feels heavy.


But all around us, people are already building something different.

GLIMMERS of Another Way of Being is a podcast about stories of grounded hope in a time of collapse.


Hosted by leadership coach and community facilitator Stephanie MacKinnon, each episode is a conversation with people who are choosing a different path—through art, community, politics, food, education, care, activism and countless other acts of everyday courage.


Together, we explore what becomes possible when we loosen the grip of the systems we've inherited and begin creating lives rooted in connection, justice and collective care.


Because another way of being isn't just possible, it's already happening.
___

If you'd like to bring a little more of the spirit of GLIMMERS into your own day-to-day, I've created a free gift for you: Your Glimmers Practice: a  20-minute guided audio and reflection workbook to help you notice the moments of hope, beauty and possibility that are already around you.


You can download it for free at:
https://stephaniemackinnon.myflodesk.com/glimmers


And if you're longing for more support as you begin creating another way of being in your own life, I'd love to welcome you into ROOTED, my coaching and community space. 


You can find out more at: https://stephaniemackinnon.com/rooted

title: The Black Line Brief
author: Matt McKim
description:
The Black Line Brief exists to examine official narratives under disciplined scrutiny. This channel applies adversarial analysis — weighing claims against evidence, incentives, omissions, and historical precedent — to matters of government action, military power, intelligence operations, institutional corruption, and public deception.
We do not begin with conclusions.
We begin with claims — and test whether they survive pressure.
This channel is not about belief.
It is about record, evidence, and accountability.
https://venmo.com/u/Matthew-McKim-2
https://buymeacoffee.com/TheBlackLineBrief

title: Dark Forms
author: Palimpsest Productions
description: Writers Jamieson Ridenhour and Andrew Clark talk with horror authors about the craft of writing dark fiction.

title: Lambeau Ladies
author: Lambeau Ladies
description: Long before NFL players had teams of trainers, nutritionists, therapists, agents, and other specialists, the dynastic Green Bay Packers of the 1960s relied on a different kind of support staff: their wives. In Lambeau Ladies, mother and daughter Laura Kostelnik Biskupic and Katherine Biskupic tell the remarkable, largely untold story of the women who helped to keep the Lombardi-era Packers performing at a championship level. From preparing game-day sandwiches, nursing injuries, and reviewing game film to managing households, hosting social gatherings, and providing emotional strength to their spouses, these women were a driving force behind one of the greatest teams in football history.

title: COM Products

Each episode, host Marcin Kubica (Head of Product at Clouds on Mars) sits down with guests and experts to explore the products and solutions being built for industries like FMCG, retail, healthcare, pharma, and real estate, as well as the broader technical and architectural challenges every modern organization has to face.
Let your data make an impact.

From courageous mice to curious bees, these unforgettable adventures help kids discover God's character while learning how to be brave in their own lives! Created for children ages 4–8 and for families to enjoy together.
Created and narrated by Jennie Bourquin.

description:
Faith and relevance in the 21st century. 
If Jesus is who he says he is in the gospels, then all of life has to centre around him. That includes our personal morality, our economics, politics, sexuality, care for the poor and for the planet. These are all central gospel issues, not optional add-ons to the “real” task of saving people’s souls. 
Soul Thoughts delves into how the gospel of Jesus is more relevant than ever in the 21st century. It does so by looking at what’s happening in our world through the lens of him.

title: Don't Stop Me Now - An Audiobook Podcast
author: Roo Bannister
description:
Plenty of you have read the very first version, here it is with my voice and intonation.


Imagine if all the men in Britain suddenly disappeared. Imagine there was no clue as to when they might return. Imagine a world where the women were suddenly called upon to do the jobs that men have dominated for decades if not centuries.

At the outbreak of World War II, that is exactly what happened! the men were gradually all called to war while the women were encouraged to go to the factory gates, the farms, the military - anywhere they could to be of service to their country. Gladly they went, while their men were off fighting, the gals did their part to keep the country going. But what of love? What of romance?

Meet Stella, a boyish young daughter of an Irish couple settled in London. Raised on the streets of London, Stella grew up in fist fights with boys in the neighbourhood, wishing she had been born a boy and feeling out of place in her time and space. At the outbreak of war, her older brother Derek was first in line to sign up for army duty, now she feels she too must serve her country as bravely as her brother. Off to the factory she goes, meeting new friends and finding independence for the first time in her life - a whole new world in war! And then...

In walks Callie, a beautiful red head with almond shaped eyes and a cheeky grin. Stella is instantly speechless, on edge and awkward whenever she encounters Callie at the factory. What on Earth are all these squiggly tummy, burning face, tongue-tied feelings all about? And what is she to do about them? The two women encounter love in the most trying of circumstances, in a world at war with itself - how even. Certainly not what Stella was raised to believe in or consider as a life choice. But....how to resist this angelic, confident and brazenly attractive young woman?

In the first part of their story, Stella and Callie struggle to battle through this new land of confusion and unspoken expectations. With no one to confide in, who do the women find the right path through life in London...and then the Lakes and later still further afield. Will they ever find a moment in time to stand together honestly and truly? Or will their discovery of love, sex and relationship in the midst of global chaos be torn apart by circumstance.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Episode 26.81 - GDTG Gender Affirmation
author: 2026 Dr. Diana Sorrentino, Ph.D.
description:
Paradoxes of Gender Episode 26.81
Counseling Adolescent Youth for Gender Affirmation Counseling Youth for Hormone Therapy Youth Gender Affirmation & Gender Emergence Stages of the Youth’s Family’s Emergence Surgical Intervention for Gender Affirmation Pathways to Gender Affirmation – Surgical & Non-Surgical Societal & Family Issues Youth Gender Emergence in Academia or the Workplace    

Episode 26.81 Guest Amber Fourman.  Amber will share her experiences with gender incongruence, gender dysphoria, and her journey into and through Gender Affirmation.  

Paradoxes of Gender Podcasts: The Paradoxes of Gender Podcasts are Designed to Deliver Professional Development Educational Programs for the LGBTQ+ Medical & Behavioral Healthcare Providers who provide Medical & Behavioral Healthcare Services to Transgender, Gender Diverse, Gender Non-Conforming, Gender Incongruent and Non-Binary Individuals.  

Prepared & Presented by: Dr. Diana Sorrentino, Ph.D. Social-Psychologist & Behavioral Analyst   Delivering comprehensive Medical or Behavioral Healthcare Services as a component of your practice on behalf of Transgender (TG), Gender Diverse (GD) Individuals and members of the LGBTQ+ Community may at times prove to be a little challenging. The challenges you may experience have historically been caused by a lack of specialized medical or behavioral health training you received in preparation for your career and the care you will be providing for Transgender and Gender Diverse patients.   

This understanding was underscored and reinforced by a 2011 study, reported in the Journal of the American Medical Association, the medical schools, on average, only provide Five (5) hours of academic or clinical instruction on LGBTQ+ healthcare requirements.  In the absence of specialized training; time is required to acquire first-hand real-world knowledge and experience with the diverse challenges, needs and experiences these individuals present and what they are confronted with as they live their lives in their Self-Attributed Non-Conforming Gender roles.  

Transgender, Gender Diverse, Gender Non-Conforming, Gender Incongruent & Non-Binary individuals, their families, friends, schools, employers and social-networks will also benefit from the content of these Podcasts in-spite of the fact that the detailed medical and psychological content of these programs focuses on the continuing educational needs and requirements of the medical or behavioral healthcare professional.  Knowledge is essential to counteract the negative publicity and the political hyperbole disseminated by individuals with no understanding of the history and underlying biological factors influencing gender non-conformity.    

Dr. Sorrentino is a Member of: 
World Professional Association for Transgender Health
GLMA – Gay Lesbian Medical Association    

Copyright 2026 – All Rights Reserved
Paradoxes of Gender Podcasts are a Professional Production of Dr. Diana Sorrentino, Ph.D.

description: This is Leverage, a podcast that focuses on international security and political economy. We, your hosts, are recent high school graduates who share a passion in understanding how different governments cooperate or oppose one another. Together with our curiosity and experience, we hope to shed light on the black box of strategies used by different states across prominent domains and regimes—and give our perspective on why it works or not. All of our information comes from reliable resources, with occasional collaborations with fellow teachers and students. While the primary purview of this podcast is international relations, our coverage also includes, but is not limited to, domestic policy and law. So settle in—let's talk leverage.

This series cuts the fluff and brings you unfiltered — and sometimes a little unhinged — conversations with women in branding, advertising and the creative industry.


We get into the bold moves that shaped them, the mistakes that taught them, and the real stories in between. Expect honesty, laughter, and plenty to learn from genuinely badass women.


Let’s be real. This industry can be tough. The pressure and the chaos. The late nights and the not knowing what’s next.


We don’t always share the truth: the lessons, the laughs, and the messy stuff in between. My hope is that by sharing stories, we can feel more seen and a little less alone.


This podcast is for the juniors figuring it out, the midweights holding it together, and the senior women leading the way and making bold moves for the future badass gals.

Save Our Sundays is the build-in-public podcast from MyTeacherAide, hosted by co-founders Josh and Jacob. Follow along as they rebuild MyTeacherAide V3, an AI toolbox designed to help teachers save time on planning, resources and admin.
Each episode goes behind the scenes of building an education technology product in real time, unpacking teacher workload, AI in education, product decisions, school feedback, curriculum alignment, privacy, and the messy reality of trying to create tools teachers actually want to use.
It’s for teachers who love teaching, but could do without spending their Sun

title: Join up with Jesse
author: Project Rescue
description: Freedom Ally Exclusive Newsletter highlights

title: War Hall
author: War Hall
description:
WAR HALL: A Theatre of the Mind 


What was built during World War 2 — and why does it still matter now?


War Hall uncovers the remarkable wartime and musical history of Walthamstow Assembly Hall — a concert venue built in London during the Blitz. It became one of Britain’s most iconic recording spaces, known for its legendary acoustics.


The series blends immersive sound design, rare archives, and performances recorded in the Hall. Its original score is created by Simon Mills — acclaimed producer, composer, and one half of the pioneering electronic duo Bent — weaving past and present into a distinctive sonic world.


Pick of the week at The Guardian.


Hosted by Angela Allen MBE - a Londoner who lived through the air raids and one of Britain’s most respected script supervisors, with a career spanning from The Third Man to The Misfits — and historian Professor John Thomas.


WAR HALL asks what was built during the war, why it mattered, and what remains at risk today. It is a story about hope.


Additional sound by Ric Peet

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Smoke and Slice Podcast
author: J.B. and Drew
description:
Brought to you by the owners of Tiger Country Smokehouse and Marceline Pizza Company: J.B. and Drew.
The most unique and deep interviews from your favorite local business owners, artists and public figures. Hear their stories like never before!
Recorded at 99.1 KDWD on Main Street USA in Walt Disney's Hometown.

title: Grind and Game
author: Chipp, CTFullmetal, DirtyDave
description:
Welcome to the Grind and Game Podcast, a weekly landing zone for gamers. We are three military veterans, husbands, and career men balancing the real-world grind of family and fitness with an unbreakable passion for gaming.
Hosted by Chris, Nick, and Dave, we provide a positive, solution-oriented look at the gaming industry. While others focus on what’s broken, we scrutinize the landscape to find the paths to success. We’re here to celebrate great games and discuss how the industry we love can keep winning!
What to expect:
Live Weekly Brief: Long-form deep dives into gaming culture, industry

title: If Question
author: Koyshon Blake
description:
What if one small change could rewrite everything you know about the world?

What if nobody needed sleep? What if money disappeared overnight? What if humans could read minds? What if Earth suddenly had two moons?

Hosted by Koyshon Blake, If Question explores fascinating thought experiments, strange possibilities, scientific mysteries, and mind-bending scenarios that challenge the way we see reality.

Each episode starts with a simple question and follows it through science, psychology, history, technology, and human behavior to discover what might actually happen.

Some questions are funny.

Some are terrifying.

Some will completely change the way you think.

Because sometimes the most impossible questions reveal the most interesting truths.

New episodes every week.

One question.

Unlimited possibilities.

title: Recovery Ecosystem Radio, an FGI Podcast
author: Recovery Ecosystem Radio
description:
Recovery Ecosystem Radio, an FGI Podcast, explores how communities can make recovery the expectation, not the exception. Hosted by Tony White, Director of Outreach for Fletcher Group, Inc. (FGI), each episode features lived experience, practical solutions, and collaborative strategies that support long-term recovery through Health, Home, Purpose, and Community. Produced by Fletcher Group, Inc., the podcast connects storytelling, research, and real-world action to help build recovery-ready communities.
www.fletchergroup.org

title: Hopper - 13 06 2026
author: Hopper
description:
This is my mix of the prettiest music.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: My Thoughts
author: Adam Jones
description: Sometimes I just get ideas in my head and I feel like I need to share what I am thinking. Not necessarily so that others can listen (although I hope you do), but so I can organize my thoughts and work through them. There is no one theme for the podcast. It can range from psychology, religion, space, really anything on my mind. Thanks for listening!

title: Murder Express
author: Murder Express
description:
Welcome aboard passenger, to the murder express. Every new trip to your destination brings a new tale of murder. 
Follow the podcast on 
INSTAGRAM: https://www.instagram.com/murderexpresspodcast?igsh=NTc4MTIwNjQ2YQ%3D%3D&utm_source=qr

title: Democratic Party Channel (DPC)
author: Yu-ai Research Center
description:
Welcome to Democratic Party Channel, the ultimate guide to Japanese politics. While the world focuses on Japan’s tech and culture, its political arena remains a fascinating enigma. We go far beyond daily headlines. 
Our episodes take a deep dive into the nation’s political journey, from crucial post-war history to current debates. We profile key figures—from historical titans to modern power brokers—and unpack the core ideologies of Japan's major parties.
Subscribe today to decode the complex history, factions, and personalities shaping Japan.

title: Gobbledygook!
author: Ragan Fox
description: Gobbledygook is a weekly bite-sized podcast from Ragan Fox exploring horror, queer culture, media, rhetoric, and the strange ways popular culture helps us understand ourselves. Equal parts cultural criticism, comedy, memoir, and academic mischief, each episode asks what happens when we take "gobbledygook" seriously.

title: Back to that Pain
author: Mike Gurgle
description: Back to That Pain is a heartfelt country ballad about the memories that never truly leave us. It's about looking back on a love that changed your life, the heartbreak that followed, and realizing that even after all this time, some wounds still have a way of finding you.Written from a place of honesty and emotion, "Back to That Pain" tells the story of revisiting the moments, places, and memories that remind us of what we've lost. It's for anyone who's ever tried to move on, only to discover that the past still has a voice.Performed by Mike Gurgle, this song is a reminder that while pain may fade with time, the people and moments that shaped us often stay in our hearts forever.

title: The Guest Ledger
author: Gabriel
description: Welcome to The Guest Ledger, where I get a new guest on each week and have unscripted conversations about anything. Tune in and listen to genuine connections form.

title: The Guest List
author: droshawn
description:
The Guest List is the go-to podcast for event planning professionals who want to stay ahead of the curve. Hosted by RSVPBook, each episode features candid conversations with top planners, venue experts, and industry innovators sharing what's actually working — from registration strategy and attendee experience to the AI tools reshaping how events get built.

Whether you're a seasoned CMP or just breaking into the industry, The Guest List delivers real insights from the people who know events best. Pull up a chair. You're on the list.

Presented by RSVPBook — the event registration platform built for professionals. Learn more at rsvpbook.com.

title: Fortified Fatherhood
author: Fernando
description: Fortified Fatherhood is a podcast for dads who lead from the front lines with discipline, integrity, and resilience. Hosted by Fernando Bernstein, each episode features honest conversations, a martial arts mindset, and practical lessons to help men become stronger fathers, better leaders, and better men.

title: BreTheScientist
author: BreTheScientist
description: Bre the Scientist explores AI, science, finance, life skills, technology, and the cosmos—connecting every part of science to everyday life through research, curiosity, and discovery.

title: Prompts from the Edge
author: Mary Madeiras and Kristy Sturdivant
description: We are on a mission to remind and reconnect humans with their hearts and creativity. AI is rapidly integrating into our lives, and we are here to prompt you to remember your authentic self.

title: Crime Vault
author: Crime Vault
description: Crime Vault uncovers the world's most chilling true crime cases, unsolved mysteries, and shocking criminal investigations. Each episode explores the evidence, the motives, and the stories behind infamous crimes with engaging storytelling and factual research.

title: SOAR: The Appalachian Blueprint
author: theappalachianblueprint
description:
SOAR: The Appalachian Blueprint is the official podcast of SOAR, featuring conversations with the leaders, innovators, entrepreneurs, educators, and community partners working to shape the future of Appalachia.

Each episode explores the ideas, initiatives, and collaborations driving economic development and creating new opportunities across Eastern Kentucky and the Appalachian region. From workforce development and entrepreneurship to healthcare, education, tourism, infrastructure, and community revitalization, SOAR: The Appalachian Blueprint highlights the people and organizations turning vision into action.

Join us as we share the stories behind the projects, partnerships, and progress building a stronger, more prosperous Appalachia—one conversation at a time.

title: LIT BUT SOBER
author: @AllieSerea
description:
The life of your dreams is on the other side of sobriety. 
From being an anxious, depressed, & insecure party girl stuck in toxic cycles + full of pain and trauma, to a now regulated, powerful, aligned, abundant & magnetic version of myself I didn't know existed, I want everyone to step into their highest self & dream life. 
 
We are getting raw and real as I guide you through how to face your pain, limited beliefs, and addictive patterns, to allow you to step into a new state of peace, joy, confidence, and radiance to attract everything you want in this life.

title: Coughlin🔒Confidential
author: Coughlin
description:
Coughlin Confidential is a conversation series exploring the moments people think they know… and the stories behind them.
Where the official story ends… and the real one begins.

title: Maybe
author: Rob McLoughlin
description:
Maybe is a podcast about possibility, the choices that change us and the questions that lead us there. 
Each episode begins with a spark, a “maybe I should” or “maybe I could” moment that opens the door to something new. 
Through solo reflections and intimate conversations, Maybe explores how uncertainty becomes transformation. 
It’s about the delicate space between doubt and determination, and the courage to turn what‑ifs into what’s next.

title: Real Crime Tales
author: Gilbert Almonte
description:
Real Crime Tales goes beyond the crime.

Every case we cover — serial killers, fugitives, cold cases, and the criminals who hid in plain sight for decades — is examined through a single lens: not just what happened, but who had the chance to stop it, and why they didn't.

These are the stories of double lives carefully constructed over years. The charming GP who ended 215 of his patients' lives. The celebrated counterculture icon who hid his girlfriend's body in a trunk for 18 months. The dating show contestant the FBI had been hunting for years. The people who fooled everyone — and the systems that let them.

Every episode is built from court records, investigative journalism, police reports, and primary sources. No speculation dressed up as fact. No exploitation. The victims stay at the center of every story we tell.

This is true crime told the right way — accurate, thorough, and unafraid to ask the harder question underneath every case: how did we get here, and who left the door open?

New episodes every week.

Want early access and the fully uncensored version of every episode — with the precise language we have to soften for other platforms? Support the show on Patreon at patreon.com/cw/realcrimetales. Three dollars a month keeps this podcast independent.

title: عـــــندما يــــكون الحــــب ألــــــم💔
author: Riquilson
description:
Fī hādhā al-mashhad, yamurru al-ithnān bi-azmatin qawiyyatin fī ʿalāqatihimā. Sīrkān yusayṭiru ʿalayhi al-ghaḍab wa al-ya's, baynamā Īdā tashʿuru bijurḥin ʿamīqin wa alamin shadīd.
Ya'tī hādhā al-mashhad baʿda an marra al-ḥabībān bil-ʿadīd mina al-khilāfāt wal-infiṣālāt wa sūʾ al-fahm wal-khasāʾir. Wa mā zālā yuḥibbāni baʿḍahumā, lākin ath-thiqah baynahumā qad tazʿazaʿat, wa yuẓhiru hādhā ash-shijār ḥajm al-alam alladhī tarākama fī qalbayhimā.

title: B*tch, Please.
author: Plant & Georgia
description: A podcast about everything. From college athletics to friendships, funny stories, and everything in between, hosts Plant and Georgia laugh, cry, bicker, and overshare, but somehow it's always a good time.

title: The FlipSide Podcast
author: Chris Sun and Ryan Kearney
description: The FlipSide Podcast breaks down US-China business, technology, AI, investing, politics, and growth strategy. Hosted by Chris Sun, a Chinese founder and Stanford GSB alum based in Silicon Valley, and Ryan Kearney, an American entrepreneur and Sichuan University MBA based in Chengdu, China. Each week, they decode Chinese companies going global, U.S. startups and venture capital, AI competition, tariffs, cross-border trade, consumer brands, and social commerce.

title: slockes 2nd test show
author: me
description: test

title: Hidden Notebooks
author: Mel Rae
description:
Hidden Notebooks is a spoken word poetry podcast by poet Mel Rae. Each episode is a single original poem read aloud, nothing more. Genuine, nostalgic, and intimate, these are the poems that live in the margins, the ones written for no one and everyone at once.
If you love spoken word poetry, poetry readings, contemporary poetry, or just words that romanticize every part of life, this is for you. 
No commentary. Just words.

title: Navigating Dementia
author: Carlyn Lenfestey
description: Navigating Dementia is a weekly podcast for anyone caring for someone with dementia, family and professional caregivers alike. Host Carlyn Lenfestey, a physical therapist and dementia care specialist with 25 years of clinical experience, uses real stories and everyday moments to show that behavior is never the problem. It's always the signal. Each episode teaches one meaningful insight through her Wheel of Function Framework™, delivered like a conversation over coffee, not a lecture. There is always a better way.

title: ADieHD - Not another ADHD Podcast
author: Adie Wyper
description: If you’ve ever felt like a Lamborghini being driven as a delivery van - this is your podcast. We’re not talking about the clinical version or the Instagram version of ADHD where everyone’s suddenly got it. We are talking about the real version that messes with your brain every single day. Hosted by Adie, a working class Scottish dad with huge ambition and blocked by thick fog because of combined ADHD. Sound familiar? This is the ADHD podcast for real people. You forgot why you walked into this room, didn’t you? Welcome home.

title: Offroad Touring Life Podcast
author: Richard
description:
Join me as I dive into the real world of 4WD touring, remote Adventures and camping.

From the vehicles we use, the camping and touring set ups and more.

With special guests who will share there expertise and experiences from decades of knowledge.

title: Generational Divide
author: BTT Studios
description: New school debates old school on the biggest topics in sports. Coach defends the veterans while Peyton Lodge argues for the modern era — one divide, no middle ground.

title: History Klaxon
author: Christine Baker
description: Want to know more about History? Subscribe to History Klaxon! This is the podcast made from the History Klaxon youtube channel. This content is created by Christine Baker, a human with a PhD in Middle Eastern History who taught university-level classes for more than decade. There will never be any AI content on History Klaxon.

title: The Athlete's Porch
author: jonkarcich
description:
Welcome to The Athlete’s Porch.
Pull up a chair. This is where athletes, coaches, and competitors talk about what the game is really like. The wins, the setbacks, the pressure, the failures, the breakthroughs, and everything in between.
Just honest conversations, lessons learned, and stories that can help the next athlete on their journey.
Because the game doesn’t just build better players—it builds better people.
Play the Long Game.
Hosted by Jon Karcich.
